emails.subject: Your Philadelphia IM assessment — Dr. Tetlow noticed a few patterns, [FIRST_NAME]
emails.preview: What your results are actually saying
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME] — quick note from Dr. Tetlow at Philadelphia Integrative Medicine.

I reviewed your Custom Health Assessment, and a few patterns came up right away:

* Primary focus: [CORE_ISSUE_1]
* Secondary contributors: [CORE_ISSUE_2], [CORE_ISSUE_3]
* Common symptoms in this pattern: [SYMPTOM_A], [SYMPTOM_B], [SYMPTOM_C]

What most care misses: answers are possible. The reason you have not had them yet is almost always because the question was never asked at the right level. Root-cause medicine means we do not accept 'normal labs, unexplained symptoms' as a conclusion. We treat it as an incomplete workup.

The 3 things that create the most meaningful improvement for people with [CORE_ISSUE_1]:

1. Identify the root — what is driving the pattern, not just producing the symptom
2. Address it with compassion and precision — restoring what the body needs to correct course
3. Track the response and adjust — until the body holds the improvement without ongoing intervention

We practice in Wayne, PA, serving the greater Philadelphia area, with in-person and telehealth options.

Your next step:
* Option A: See your recommended first steps inside the app → [VIEW_MY_PLAN_LINK]
* Option B: Book a new patient visit — real answers start with a real intake → [SCHEDULE_LINK]

— Dr. Georgia Tetlow, MD ABOIM ABPMR IFMCP
Philadelphia Integrative Medicine | Wayne, PA | philly-im.com

emails.subject: Why [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] often resists treatment — the mechanism explained
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

I want to explain something about persistent symptoms that I find most patients have never been told.

The body has a remarkable capacity for healing — but that capacity depends on inputs the body is not getting, or on systemic disruptions that have not been identified and cleared. When neither has been addressed, symptoms persist regardless of how hard the patient is working to improve.

For a pattern like yours — with [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] as a central complaint — the mechanism usually involves one or more of the following: a chronic inflammatory burden that has become its own driver, hormonal or neurotransmitter dysregulation that compounds over time, or a nutrient depletion pattern that affects multiple systems simultaneously.

These do not produce dramatic symptoms that trigger diagnostic thresholds. They produce a persistent, pervasive feeling that something is off — but nothing 'shows' on a standard panel.

Integrative and functional medicine is designed to find those drivers. I trained in both physical medicine and functional medicine precisely because the most effective care addresses the whole person — not just the most testable piece.

That is what we offer at Philadelphia Integrative Medicine.

— Dr. Georgia Tetlow, MD ABOIM ABPMR IFMCP
Philadelphia Integrative Medicine | Wayne, PA | philly-im.com

[Book a New Patient Visit →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

emails.subject: A patient with your pattern — what changed when we asked the right questions
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

A brief patient story before I close the loop on your assessment.

A woman in her mid-40s came to our practice after years of musculoskeletal pain, persistent fatigue, and what she described as 'losing herself' — her energy, her focus, her sense of ease in her own body. Conventional workups had found nothing actionable.

After a full integrative intake — combining my rehabilitation medicine background with a functional panel — we identified a pattern of autoimmune activity at a subclinical level, a significant inflammatory burden, and a hormonal environment that was technically 'normal' but clearly not optimal for her.

We built a personalized care plan: targeted anti-inflammatory protocols, hormonal support, and a structured rehabilitation approach that addressed how the body was holding stress physically.

Eight months in, she described her pain as 'manageable for the first time in years' and said she felt like she had 'gotten her life back.'

Individual results vary. Outcomes depend on many personal factors and are not guaranteed. But the consistent thread across patients with patterns like yours is this: real answers exist when the right questions are asked.

Let us ask them.

— Dr. Georgia Tetlow, MD ABOIM ABPMR IFMCP
Philadelphia Integrative Medicine | Wayne, PA | philly-im.com

Disclaimer: Individual results vary. This is not medical advice.

[Schedule Your New Patient Visit →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

🔎 DIGITAL SEO HEALTH REPORT — philly-im.com
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 DOMAIN SNAPSHOT
──────────────────
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Global Rank | #734,856 |
| Keywords Ranked | 1,903 |
| Monthly Traffic | ~1,698 visits |
| Traffic Value | $1,859/mo |
| Keywords in Top 10 | 12 |
| SERP Features Traffic | ~0 visits/mo |

---

🔑 THE CORE FINDING: RANKINGS EXIST — TRAFFIC DOESN'T FOLLOW
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Philadelphia Integrative Medicine ranks for 1,903 keywords but converts almost none of that visibility into clicks. Here's the breakdown:

- **Position #1:** 1 keyword
- **Positions #2–3:** 2 keywords
- **Positions #4–10:** 9 keywords
- **Positions #11–20:** 38 keywords

Nearly 80% of ranked keywords sit between positions 11–20 — the digital waiting room where almost no one clicks. The practice has real keyword authority; it simply hasn't been pushed over the finish line.

---

🤖 AI SEARCH GAP
──────────────────
Philadelphia Integrative Medicine appears in **34 AI Overview SERPs** but is cited in **0** of them. That means patients are receiving AI-generated answers on topics this practice ranks for — and a competitor, not PIM, is getting the attribution. With AI search accelerating, zero citations across 34 triggered SERPs is an urgent credibility gap.

---

🎯 BIGGEST OPPORTUNITY: LOCAL & SPECIALTY CARE SEARCH CLUSTER
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The highest-volume, lowest-competition keywords are hiding in plain sight:

- [pos 20] *osteopath close to me* — 2,900/mo | KD:11
- [pos 12] *osteopath close to me* — 2,900/mo | KD:11
- [pos 5] *naturopathic doctor philadelphia* — 210/mo | KD:30
- [pos 8] *colon cleanse philadelphia pa* — 260/mo | KD:10
- [pos 1] *philadelphia integrative medicine* — 390/mo | KD:36
- [pos 20] *colonic philadelphia* — 390/mo | KD:0

'Osteopath close to me' alone pulls 2,900 searches monthly at a difficulty of just 11 — moving from position 20 to the top 3 on a near-zero-competition term is the single fastest path to meaningful new patient traffic.

---

⚡ WITHIN STRIKING DISTANCE (pos 4–10)
──────────────────────────────────────
- [pos 9] *power2patient* — 2,400/mo | KD:17
- [pos 10] *is cranberry juice good for your liver* — 480/mo | KD:17
- [pos 8] *colon cleanse philadelphia pa* — 260/mo | KD:10
- [pos 5] *naturopathic doctor philadelphia* — 210/mo | KD:30
- [pos 6] *how to get rid of mold in your sinuses* — 210/mo | KD:16
- [pos 5] *smashed fish* — 210/mo | KD:13

→ **Highest priority:** *naturopathic doctor philadelphia* at position 5 with KD:30 — a direct new-patient search term one or two positions away from the top-3 click zone.

---

🏆 VS YOUR TOP COMPETITOR
──────────────────────────
| Metric | philly-im.com | theholistichealing.org |
|---|---|---|
| Keywords Ranked | 1,903 | 659 |
| Monthly Traffic | ~1,698 | ~216 |
| Traffic Value | $1,859/mo | $181/mo |

PIM outranks its closest competitor on every measurable dimension — the opportunity isn't to catch up, it's to pull further ahead before the gap closes.

---

🎯 3-PRIORITY ATTACK PLAN
──────────────────────────
**1. CONVERT THE 38 PAGE-2 KEYWORDS TO PAGE 1**
Strengthen existing content targeting positions 11–20; 38 keywords moving one page forward would materially shift the ~1,698 monthly baseline. *Target: 15+ new page-1 rankings within 90 days.*

**2. CAPTURE 'OSTEOPATH CLOSE TO ME' (2,900/mo, KD:11)**
Build a dedicated local OMM landing page optimized for near-me intent. This is the highest-volume, lowest-competition term in the entire portfolio. *Target: top-5 position within 60 days.*

**3. EARN AI OVERVIEW CITATIONS (0 of 34)**
Restructure the 34 overlapping content pages with direct-answer formatting so AI systems can attribute PIM as the source. *Target: cited in 5+ AI Overviews within 90 days.*

gamma_content.ad_callouts_slide:
## Ad Extensions & Callouts
**Philadelphia Integrative Medicine**

---

### Sitelink Extensions
| Label | Destination |
|---|---|
| New Patient Info | /new-patients/ |
| Conditions We Treat | /conditions-we-treat/ |
| Meet Our Providers | /our-practitioners/ |
| Specialty Lab Testing | /specialty-testing/ |
| Pricing & Superbill | /pricing-reimbursement/ |
| Patient Success Stories | /success-stories/ |

---

### Callout Extensions
- 8x Best Integrative Practice on the Main Line
- IFM-Certified & Fellowship-Trained Providers
- Superbill Provided for Reimbursement
- Collaborative Team-Based Care Model
- Root-Cause Focused Treatment Plans
- Specialty Functional Lab Testing Available
- Serving Patients Since 2009
- In-Person & Virtual Appointments Available

---

### Why These Extensions Matter
Callout extensions increase ad real estate and click-through rate by an average of 10–15%. Sitelinks drive qualified users directly to conversion pages — bypassing the homepage entirely and reducing drop-off. For a practice with a $675–$995 new patient visit, every incremental click to the booking page is a high-value conversion opportunity.

app_config.tagline: Root-Cause Medicine. Compassionate Care. Real Answers.
symptoms: Persistent fatigue that does not improve with rest
Unexplained weight gain or difficulty losing weight
Digestive issues such as bloating, IBS, or SIBO
Joint or muscle pain without clear diagnosis
Skin conditions such as eczema or psoriasis
History of mold exposure or mold illness
Adrenal fatigue or chronic stress response
Heart palpitations or unexplained cardiovascular symptoms
Headaches or migraines without clear cause
Low libido or sexual health concerns

careModels.name: New Patient Comprehensive Visit
careModels.desc: An in-depth first visit with a fellowship-trained, IFM-certified provider. Full health history review, specialty testing recommendations, and a personalized integrative care plan.

careModels.name: Follow-Up Consultation
careModels.desc: Ongoing care visit to review lab results, refine your treatment plan, and address evolving health goals with your provider.

careModels.name: Follow-Up Package — 4 Visits with NP/PA
careModels.desc: A bundled package of four follow-up appointments with our nurse practitioner or physician assistant. Ideal for ongoing chronic condition management and cost-effective continuity of care.

careModels.name: Osteopathic Manipulative Medicine (OMM)
careModels.desc: Hands-on osteopathic treatment from a board-certified osteopathic physician. Addresses pain, structural imbalance, and nervous system dysregulation as part of a whole-body integrative plan.

careModels.name: Specialty Functional Lab Testing
careModels.desc: Advanced diagnostic testing including microbiome analysis, heavy metal panels, genetic and nutrigenomic testing, environmental toxin screening, and comprehensive hormone panels.
